final_text: Acme Analytics, Inc. is a B2B SaaS company providing analytics dashboards for mid-market finance teams. The company has 42 employees, operates fully remote with no physical offices, and hosts customer data on AWS (us-east-1 primary, us-west-2 for disaster recovery). Production and staging environments run in separate AWS accounts. (Company Profile)
one_line_note: Every claim matches STRONG evidence from Company Profile; citation verified. No overclaim.

final_text: Acme holds a SOC 2 Type II report issued March 2026, covering January through December 2025, with no exceptions noted. ISO 27001 is not held. (SOC 2 Type II Report, 2026-03)
one_line_note: Both claims match STRONG evidence: SOC 2 report dates/no exceptions and ISO 27001 not held. Citation verified; no overclaim.

final_text: Customer data at rest is encrypted with AES-256 using AWS KMS, with keys rotated annually via automatic rotation (Encryption Policy v2.3 §4, §4.2). Data in transit is protected with TLS 1.2 minimum on all public endpoints; TLS 1.3 is preferred where supported (Encryption Policy v2.3 §5). Customer-managed keys (CMK) are not addressed in our current documentation — NEEDS OWNER INPUT: confirm whether CMK is supported, and if so, describe the mechanism.
one_line_note: REVISED: Confidence downgraded from 🟢 to 🟡. Evidence-backed claims are accurate and cited, but the NEEDS OWNER INPUT sub-question (CMK) has zero evidence — a 🟢 tag on the full answer would mislead a reviewer into thinking every part is evidenced. 🟡 honestly signals the mixed confidence.

final_text: Yes — SSO via Okta with MFA is enforced for all employees and contractors. (Access Control Policy v1.8 §2)
one_line_note: Exact match to STRONG evidence from Access Control Policy §2; citation verified. No overclaim.

final_text: Access reviews are conducted quarterly. Upon employee termination, access is revoked within 24 hours (Access Control Policy v1.8 §5). Role-based access follows least-privilege principles, and production access is limited to on-call engineers via short-lived credentials (Access Control Policy v1.8 §3).
one_line_note: All four sub-claims match STRONG evidence from two policy sections; citations verified. No overclaim.

final_text: Penetration testing is performed annually. The most recent test was conducted in November 2025 by Redwood Security. No critical findings were identified; two high-severity findings were remediated within 30 days. (Pen Test Executive Summary, 2025-11)
one_line_note: All claims match STRONG evidence from the pen test executive summary; citation verified. No overclaim.

final_text: Log retention is set at 12 months. AWS CloudTrail and application logs are shipped to Datadog with alerting configured for authentication anomalies and privilege changes (Logging & Monitoring Standard v1.2). Whether retention can be extended beyond 12 months per customer request is not addressed in our current documentation — NEEDS OWNER INPUT: confirm whether extended log retention is supported and the mechanism for enabling it.
one_line_note: REVISED: Confidence downgraded from 🟢 to 🟡. The evidenced portion (12-month retention, Datadog, alerting) is correct and cited, but the extension sub-question is a 🔴 gap. 🟢 on the whole answer overstates confidence.

final_text: Yes, a documented incident response plan is maintained with a severity matrix and on-call rotation (Incident Response Plan v2.0). Affected customers are notified within 72 hours of a confirmed breach involving their data (Incident Response Plan v2.0 §6).
one_line_note: Both claims match STRONG evidence from IR plan and §6 Notification; citations verified. No overclaim.

final_text: Daily encrypted backups are performed with cross-region replication to us-west-2. Target RTO is 8 hours and target RPO is 24 hours (BC/DR Summary v1.1). The date and results of the most recent disaster recovery test are not documented in our current corpus — NEEDS OWNER INPUT: provide the most recent DR test date and pass/fail results.
one_line_note: REVISED: Confidence downgraded from 🟢 to 🟡. Backup/RTO/RPO claims are 🟢-worthy and cited, but the DR test date sub-question is a 🔴 gap. 🟡 accurately reflects the mix.

final_text: Background checks are performed on all new hires (Employee Handbook §7). The screening vendor and scope of checks performed are not documented in our current corpus — NEEDS OWNER INPUT: provide the background-check vendor name and the scope of checks (criminal, credit, etc.).
one_line_note: REVISED: Confidence downgraded from 🟢 to 🟡. The background-check claim is 🟢-worthy and cited, but the vendor/scope sub-question is a 🔴 gap. 🟡 honestly signals partial evidence.

final_text: Endpoint protections include Microsoft Intune MDM on all devices, full-disk encryption (BitLocker on Windows, FileVault on macOS), and Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (EDR) deployed fleet-wide. (Endpoint Standard v1.0)
one_line_note: All three claims match STRONG evidence from Endpoint Standard; citation verified. No overclaim.

final_text: Current subprocessors with access to customer data: AWS (hosting), Datadog (logging/monitoring), Stripe (billing), Google Workspace (email/docs), and Zendesk (support) (Subprocessor List, 2026-01). A Data Processing Agreement is available on request and incorporates GDPR Standard Contractual Clauses (Privacy Summary).
one_line_note: Subprocessor list is an exact match to STRONG evidence; DPA claim matches Privacy Summary. Citations verified; no overclaim.

final_text: Acme does not have a dedicated Data Protection Officer appointed. A DPA is available on request and incorporates GDPR Standard Contractual Clauses. CCPA requests can be submitted to privacy@acme.example, with the privacy policy available at acme.example/privacy. (Privacy Summary)
one_line_note: All four sub-claims match STRONG evidence from Privacy Summary; citation verified. No overclaim, no false confidence.
